cards.title: NVIDIA Nemotron Post-Training v3 Prompt Atlas: Interactive Map of 10T+ Agent Training Tokens
cards.why_it_matters: As an AI/research engineer building or evaluating agents, this gives you a visual, filterable lens into the exact prompt mixtures and curation choices behind a major open post-training dataset — directly actionable for data selection, eval design, and understanding model behavior.
cards.summary: NVIDIA's Nemotron open data release covers 10+ trillion pre-training tokens and millions of post-training samples. The new Prompt Atlas is an interactive semantic map where each point is a post-training prompt, volume-sampled to reflect true data mixture proportions, with filters by dataset, pipeline stage, domain, and tool-use. Nearly 145 ICML papers cite Nemotron models or datasets.

cards.title: Jailbreak: LLM-Synthesized Storage Readers Bypass Database Engines, Achieve Up to 27× Speedup
cards.why_it_matters: An AI infrastructure engineer will find this directly relevant as a concrete demonstration of agentic code synthesis for systems-level performance — LLMs reading format specs to generate Arrow-compatible readers that replace JDBC/ODBC entirely.
cards.summary: Jailbreak uses LLMs to synthesize storage-format-specific readers from database source code and documentation, bypassing query engines entirely and materializing data as Apache Arrow columnar buffers. Evaluated on PostgreSQL and MySQL with TPC-H benchmarks, generated readers are validated for correctness against JDBC/ODBC baselines and achieve up to 27× speedup in analytical throughput, compatible with DuckDB, Spark, cuDF, and Spark RAPIDS.

cards.title: Hugging Face's Transformers Backend for vLLM Now Matches Native Speed via torch.fx Graph Rewriting
cards.why_it_matters: For an ML engineer deploying models in production, this means any Hugging Face model — including Qwen3 MoE at 235B FP8 — can run in vLLM at near-native speed with a single flag, eliminating the need to write custom vLLM ports.
cards.summary: The updated transformers modeling backend for vLLM uses torch.fx for static graph analysis and AST rewriting to apply layer fusions and optimized kernels at runtime, closing the performance gap with hand-written vLLM implementations. Benchmarks cover Qwen3-4B (single GPU), 32B (tensor parallel), and 235B-A22B-FP8 MoE (data+expert parallel on 8×H100). Activated via --model-impl transformers.

cards.title: SciReasoner: Multimodal Foundation Model Achieves SOTA on 67 of 86 Structure-Property Benchmarks
cards.why_it_matters: A researcher interested in multimodal architectures and interpretable reasoning will want to study how SciReasoner's structure-aware tokenization and chain-of-thought traces achieve both SOTA accuracy and expert-preferred explanations across proteins, molecules, and crystals.
cards.summary: SciReasoner discretizes protein coordinates, molecular topologies, and crystal connectivities into a unified structure-aware vocabulary, enabling inspectable reasoning. It improves GO Cellular Component F-max from 0.42 to 0.55 for low-homology proteins and single-step retrosynthesis accuracy from 0.63 to 0.72. Across 86 benchmarks it reaches SOTA on 67; expert evaluators preferred or found comparable its reasoning traces in 98% of double-blind cases.

cards.title: CO-LMLM: Continuous-Query Limited Memory LM Beats Models Trained on 40x More Data at 360M Scale
cards.why_it_matters: This is a technically distinct pretraining paradigm — externalizing factual knowledge to a KB with continuous vector keys — that achieves strong factual precision without scaling parameters, relevant to anyone working on retrieval-augmented or knowledge-controlled LMs.
cards.summary: CO-LMLM pairs continuous vector keys with textual KB values, generating flexible queries at low cost while keeping retrieved knowledge human-readable. An annotation pipeline tags factual spans in arbitrary text, removing prior Wikipedia-only restrictions. At 360M parameters, it achieves lower perplexity than models pretrained on 40x more data and SimpleQA performance comparable to gpt-4o-mini and above Claude Sonnet 4.5.

cards.title: Analysis-Driven Transformer Linearization Scales to 32B: Sink Tokens and Cache Routing Close the Gap
cards.why_it_matters: Researchers working on efficient long-context inference will find the theoretical dissection of why delta-style state updates outperform gated accumulation, plus the structural interventions, directly useful for designing or evaluating linear attention replacements.
cards.summary: In a frozen-backbone linearization regime, the paper shows softmax attention relies on key-dependent rank-1 orthogonal projections, explaining delta-style networks' advantage over purely gated accumulation. Structural interventions — sink tokens, short convolutions, and fixed-budget cache routing — reduce approximation errors. Applied to LLaMA and Qwen models up to 32B, the approach outperforms prior post-hoc linearization baselines on MMLU and matches complex adaptive-caching frameworks on long-context retrieval.

cards.title: STRACE: Causal Trajectory Filtering for Agent Optimization Boosts Formal Verification Success 42.5%→58.5%
cards.why_it_matters: For engineers working on LLM-based agent optimization and reflection loops, STRACE directly addresses the noisy-trace problem by combining batch-level failure pattern mining with within-trace causal localization — a practical system with strong empirical gains.
cards.summary: STRACE builds a textual dependency graph over agent execution traces, using causal localization to strip non-causal steps and identify root-cause modules, while at batch level mining failure patterns to filter redundant traces. On the VeruSAGE-Bench formal verification task, it improves success rate from 42.5% to 58.5% (1.4× improvement) over human-expert designed agents, outperforming standard context-filtering baselines.
